text: PitchVision: AI-Powered Football Match Analysis and Tactical Insights Ripu Daman Singh Bankawat b23cs1057@iitj.ac.in Mayank Vishwakarma b23cm1023@iitj.ac.in 1 Introduction The rapid growth of Artificial Intelligence in sports analytics has transformed how teams, an- alysts, and fans understand football matches. Modern football clubs increasingly rely on com- puter vision systems to analyze player positioning, tactical structures, and team performance. However, building such systems requires solv

positioning, tactical structures, and team performance. However, building such systems requires solving several challenging computer vision problems, including object detection, classification, spatial reasoning, and tactical analysis. PitchVision aims to introduce students to these concepts by developing an AI-powered football match analysis system capable of extracting meaningful information from football match images. Starting from basic object detection, the project gradually progresses towa

m football match images. Starting from basic object detection, the project gradually progresses toward higher- level tactical understanding and insight generation. The project combines deep learning, computer vision, machine learning, and data analytics into a single end-to-end application, making it an excellent learning opportunity for students interested in AI and sports technology. 2 Problem Statement Given a still image captured from a football match, develop a computer vision system capabl

tatement Given a still image captured from a football match, develop a computer vision system capable of: • Detecting all players, referees, and the football. • Classifying players into their respective teams. • Estimating player locations on the field. • Inferring team formations and tactical structures. • Generating basic football analytics and tactical insights from detected player positions. The long-term vision is to create a foundation for advanced football analytics systems that can event

he long-term vision is to create a foundation for advanced football analytics systems that can eventually process videos and generate comprehensive match statistics. 3 Project Objectives 3.1 Objective 1: Football Object Detection Develop a deep learning model capable of identifying all relevant entities present in a football match image. Detected objects include: • Players 1

• Goalkeepers • Referees • Football The system should accurately localize these entities using bounding boxes and confidence scores. 3.2 Objective 2: Team Classification After detecting players, the system should automatically separate them into their respective teams. Possible approaches include: • Jersey color extraction • Dominant color analysis • K-Means clustering • Feature-based classification methods The final output should assign every detected player to Team A or Team B. 3.3 Objective 3

on methods The final output should assign every detected player to Team A or Team B. 3.3 Objective 3: Pitch Understanding To perform tactical analysis, player positions must be mapped onto a football field representa- tion. Tasks include: • Detecting field boundaries and markings • Estimating pitch orientation • Mapping player locations onto a top-down tactical view • Identifying attacking and defending directions This stage introduces concepts such as perspective transformation and geometric co

ending directions This stage introduces concepts such as perspective transformation and geometric computer vision. 3.4 Objective 4: Formation Detection Using player positions, the system will estimate team formations. Examples include: • 4-3-3 • 4-4-2 • 4-2-3-1 • 3-5-2 Formation estimation will be based on player clustering and positional analysis. The output will include both visual and textual descriptions of detected formations. 2

3.5 Objective 5: Tactical Insights and Analytics Once player positions and formations have been identified, the system will generate basic tactical insights. Potential metrics include: • Team width • Team compactness • Defensive line positioning • Midfield density • Player distribution across the pitch These analytics provide a first step toward automated football intelligence systems. 4 Technical Approach The project will be implemented using a modular pipeline. 4.1 Stage 1: Detection • YOLO-ba

Approach The project will be implemented using a modular pipeline. 4.1 Stage 1: Detection • YOLO-based object detector • Transfer learning on football datasets • Bounding box prediction and confidence estimation 4.2 Stage 2: Classification • Color extraction from detected player regions • Team clustering using unsupervised learning • Referee filtering 4.3 Stage 3: Spatial Mapping • OpenCV-based field processing • Perspective transformation • Coordinate normalization 4.4 Stage 4: Formation Analy

ield processing • Perspective transformation • Coordinate normalization 4.4 Stage 4: Formation Analysis • Player position clustering • Line detection and spatial grouping • Formation matching algorithms 3

4.5 Stage 5: Insight Generation • Statistical analysis of player distributions • Tactical metric computation • Dashboard and visual reporting 5 Technology Stack Programming Language • Python Deep Learning • PyTorch • Ultralytics YOLO Computer Vision • OpenCV Machine Learning • Scikit-Learn Data Visualization • Matplotlib • Seaborn Development Environment • Jupyter Notebook • VS Code Version Control • Git and GitHub 6 Expected Deliverables By the end of the project, participants should produce: 1

l • Git and GitHub 6 Expected Deliverables By the end of the project, participants should produce: 1. Football object detection model. 2. Team classification module. 3. Tactical field visualization system. 4. Formation detection pipeline. 5. Basic football analytics dashboard. 6. Complete documentation and final report. 7. Demonstration showcasing end-to-end analysis on football match images. 4

7 Learning Outcomes Participants will gain practical experience in: • Deep Learning Fundamentals • Object Detection Models • Transfer Learning • Computer Vision with OpenCV • Unsupervised Learning and Clustering • Geometric Transformations • Data Visualization • End-to-End AI System Design • Sports Analytics Applications The project is designed to expose students to both theoretical concepts and real-world AI engineering practices. 8 Project Milestones 1. Dataset collection and football object d

eal-world AI engineering practices. 8 Project Milestones 1. Dataset collection and football object detection. 2. Player and team classification. 3. Pitch mapping and tactical visualization. 4. Formation estimation. 5. Analytics generation and dashboard creation. 6. Final integration, testing, and presentation. 9 Future Scope This project serves as a foundation for more advanced football analytics systems. Future ex- tensions may include: • Player tracking across video sequences • Ball trajectory

systems. Future ex- tensions may include: • Player tracking across video sequences • Ball trajectory prediction • Pass network generation • Possession analysis • Heatmap generation • Event detection (passes, shots, fouls) • Expected Goals (xG) estimation • Real-time match analysis 5

• Automated scouting and performance reports Ultimately, the project can evolve toward a comprehensive AI-powered football analytics platform capable of generating rich tactical and statistical insights from full match footage. 6
